name: session-init
description: Initializes session with environment check and task status overview. Use when user mentions セッション開始, 作業開始, 状況確認, what should I work on, start session. Do NOT load for: 実装作業, レビュー, セッション途中の作業.
capabilities: Displays current git branch and recent commits
Reads and summarizes Plans.md task status
Checks AGENTS.md for role assignments and restrictions
Monitors file sizes for Plans.md and session-log.md
Integrates with Claude-mem for context awareness
author: Chachamaru127
securitySummary: This skill performs only read-only operations including git status checks, Plans.md reading, and file size monitoring. All bash commands are informational queries with hardcoded arguments. No network activity, code execution, or sensitive data access. Static findings are false positives triggered by documentation examples and metadata field values.
valueStatement: Start your coding sessions with a complete status overview. This skill automatically checks your git status, reviews pending tasks from Plans.md, and summarizes key information from AGENTS.md.
limitations: Read-only operations only - no file modifications
Requires existing Plans.md and AGENTS.md files
No network requests or external API calls

useCases.title: Daily development session start
useCases.description: Quickly understand your current work status, pending tasks, and project context when starting your coding day.

useCases.title: Team status overview
useCases.description: Get immediate visibility into ongoing tasks, team assignments, and project progress without manual file checking.

useCases.title: Context switching aid
useCases.description: Resume work seamlessly after breaks by instantly seeing where you left off and what needs attention.

promptTemplates.title: Start morning session
promptTemplates.prompt: セッション開始 - show me what I need to work on today

promptTemplates.title: Check project status
promptTemplates.prompt: 状況を確認して - give me a status update on current tasks

promptTemplates.title: Resume after break
promptTemplates.prompt: what should I work on? I need to catch up on pending tasks

promptTemplates.title: Team sync preparation
promptTemplates.prompt: 作業開始 - summarize current branch status and ongoing work for team sync

bestPractices: Use at the start of each work session to maintain context awareness
Keep Plans.md updated for accurate task tracking
Review AGENTS.md regularly to understand project constraints
antiPatterns: Using for mid-session work - this is for initialization only
Relying on it without maintaining Plans.md properly
Ignoring file size warnings about large log files

faq.question: Is this compatible with all Claude Code versions?
faq.answer: Yes, it uses standard bash commands and file operations that work across all Claude Code versions.

faq.question: What are the file size limits?
faq.answer: Plans.md warns at 200+ lines, session-log.md at 500+ lines. These are suggestions, not hard limits.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my existing workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, it reads standard files (Plans.md, AGENTS.md) and works with any git repository.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: The skill only reads files and performs git commands - no data is sent externally or modified.

faq.question: What if Plans.md does not exist?
faq.answer: The skill will note it is missing and suggest running /harness-init to create it.

faq.question: How does Claude-mem integration work?
faq.answer: If Claude-mem is configured in harness mode, it displays past context and session summaries automatically.
